info.title: Ploi Cloud API
info.description: Ploi Cloud API for managing containerized applications on Kubernetes clusters. Use API keys to authenticate and manage applications, services, deployments, and more.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of applications for the current team. Applications can be filtered by status
and searched by name. The response includes basic application information along with associated domains
and services.

post.description:
Create a new containerized application in the current team. The application will be deployed to the cloud
platform with automatic SSL certificate provisioning. Laravel applications receive default build and init
commands automatically. WordPress applications can either use a Git repository or a persistent volume for
file storage.

get.description:
Retrieve comprehensive information about a specific application including its configuration, domains,
services, and secrets. Only accessible to team members with appropriate permissions.

put.description:
Update application configuration including name, build commands, and initialization commands.
Modifying build or init commands will automatically mark the application as needing deployment.
Changes to commands will take effect on the next deployment.

delete.description:
Permanently delete an application and all associated resources including services, deployments,
secrets, and platform resources. This action cannot be undone. The application environment
will be removed from the platform.

post.description:
Initiate a new deployment for the application. This will trigger a build process that clones the
repository, builds an image, and deploys it to the platform. Any pending or running deployments
will be automatically cancelled. The deployment runs asynchronously and progress can be tracked
via the deployments endpoint.

When skip_build is true, the deployment will reuse the container image from the most recent
successful deployment instead of building a new one. This is useful for quick redeployments
when only configuration changes are needed. Returns 422 if no previous successful deployment exists.

Rollback an application to a previous successful deployment by rebuilding from its commit SHA.
This triggers a full rebuild targeting the specific commit rather than reusing an old image.

get.description:
Export the current application configuration as a YAML file compatible with the
infrastructure apply endpoint. The exported YAML includes application settings,
repository configuration, runtime, commands, services, secrets, domains, and volumes.

This YAML can be re-imported via `POST /api/v1/infrastructure/apply` to recreate
or update the application configuration.

enum: Autoscaling is not enabled for this application
Unable to retrieve metrics from cluster

get.description:
Retrieve the current build and initialization commands for the application. Build commands
run during image construction, while init commands run each time the application starts.

patch.description:
Update the build and initialization commands for the application. Build commands execute
during image build with root permissions. Init commands run with application user
permissions (UID/GID 33) before starting the main process. Commands are executed sequentially
and stop on first failure. Updating commands marks the application as needing deployment.

Configure PHP version, extensions, and runtime settings for the application. Changes require
a new deployment to take effect. PHP settings are applied via custom php.ini configuration.
Extensions are installed using the install-php-extensions tool during build.

get.description:
Retrieve all running pod instances for an application. Returns both web instances
and scheduler instances (if scheduler is enabled). Each instance can be used
as a target for command execution.

post.description:
Execute a command in a running instance. The command is executed via the container's
shell and the output is returned. All command executions are logged for audit purposes.
Commands have a 120-second timeout.

get.description:
Retrieve paginated command execution history for an application. Shows all commands
that have been executed, including their status, output, and who executed them.
Results are ordered by execution time (newest first).

Retrieve comprehensive debug information for an application including build issues,
instance issues, deployment problems, and recommendations.
Get a quick summary of the application's health status with critical issue count.

Retrieve all custom domains associated with an application. Does not include the primary
application domain (e.g., app-name.test.ploi.it). Each domain automatically receives an
SSL certificate via Let's Encrypt after deployment.

post.description:
Add a custom domain to the application. The domain must be properly configured with DNS
pointing to the platform's load balancer before adding. SSL certificates are automatically
provisioned via Let's Encrypt during the next deployment, unless `force_apply` is set
to `true` — in which case the domain is activated and SSL provisioning begins immediately
without requiring a deployment.

delete.description:
Remove a custom domain from the application. By default the application must be
redeployed for the change to take effect. Set `force_apply` to `true` to immediately
update routing — if this was the last custom domain, the application's preview domain
is automatically restored.

get.description:
Retrieve the SSL certificate status for a specific domain. Shows certificate issuer,
expiration date, and whether auto-renewal is enabled. Certificates are managed by
cert-manager and automatically renewed 30 days before expiration.

enum: Network memberships updated. NetworkPolicy will be updated on next deployment.

get.description:
Retrieve all environment variables configured for an application. Secret values are always
masked as '********' in API responses for security. Secrets are encrypted at rest in the
database. Changes to secrets require a new deployment to take effect.

post.description:
Add a new environment variable to the application. Secret keys must be uppercase letters,
numbers, and underscores only. Values are encrypted before storage. All secrets are
passed as build arguments during image construction and injected as environment variables
at runtime.

put.description:
Update the value of an existing environment variable. The key cannot be changed - to rename
a secret, delete and recreate it. Updating a secret automatically marks the application as
needing deployment. The new value will be encrypted before storage.

delete.description:
Remove an environment variable from the application. This action cannot be undone.
Deleting a secret automatically marks the application as needing deployment. The
secret will be removed from the application environment on the next deployment.

get.description:
Retrieve environment variables automatically injected by application services such as
databases, caches, and other services. These are not user-defined secrets but are
generated by the platform based on the services attached to the application. Values
are shown in plain text as they are not user secrets.

Retrieve all services associated with an application. Services include databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB),
caches (Redis, Valkey), message queues (RabbitMQ), object storage (MinIO), background workers, and SFTP servers.
Each service runs in the same application environment and automatically injects environment
variables into the main application.

post.description:
Add a new service to an application. Services run as separate services in the same environment and
automatically inject connection details as environment variables. Database services create persistent
volumes for data storage. Worker services share the same application image and volumes as the main application.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a service and all associated data. This will remove the service,
persistent volumes, and any stored data. This action cannot be undone. The service will be
marked for deletion and removed asynchronously.

put.description:
Update configuration for an existing service. Only certain settings can be modified after creation.
Changes may require a service restart to take effect. Worker services can update their command,
while other services have limited update capabilities.

post.description:
Restart a service. This causes a brief downtime
while the service restarts. Useful for applying configuration changes or recovering from issues.

Create temporary external access to a service for debugging purposes. This creates an external
service with a random port that allows direct connection from outside the platform. Access
automatically expires after the specified duration. Useful for database administration or
troubleshooting.

delete.description:
Remove debug access to a service. This deletes the temporary external service and revokes
external access. Any active connections will be terminated.

get.description:
Retrieve the environment variables that this service automatically injects into the main
application. These variables provide connection details and credentials for
accessing the service from the application.

Retrieve available connection string formats for a service with debug access enabled.
Supports multiple formats including standard URIs, JDBC connections, and CLI commands
for different database types.
get.summary: Get connection formats for a service

patch.description: Update various application settings including health checks and scheduler configuration.

Update resource allocation settings for an application. This endpoint requires a RAM value
and automatically calculates CPU allocation based on the predefined RAM_TO_CPU_MAP.
Changes require a new deployment to take effect.
Update the repository configuration for an application.

get.description: Get detailed status information including SSL certificate status and deployment readiness.

post.description: Dismiss getting started or deployment needed notifications.

get.description:
Download the source archive for an application. This endpoint uses signed URLs
for secure access by build pods during the deployment process.

enum: Failed to convert zip to tar.gz.
Invalid zip archive: contains unsafe file paths.
Invalid file type. Only .zip and .tar.gz files are accepted.

post.description:
Upload a source archive (.zip or .tar.gz) for an application that uses upload-based deployments
instead of a Git repository. The uploaded archive will be used as the source code for the next deployment.

get.description:
Retrieve all volumes associated with an application. This includes both service volumes
(from databases, caches, etc.) and custom application volumes. Service volumes have
type: 'service' while custom volumes have type: 'application'.

post.description:
Add a custom volume to the application. The volume will be created as a persistent
volume claim and mounted at the specified path. Creating a volume marks the
application as needing deployment.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific volume including size, usage,
and status. The volumeId parameter can be either the PVC name for service volumes
(e.g., "db-pvc") or the numeric ID for application volumes.

patch.description:
Resize a volume to a larger size. The resize operation is performed asynchronously
and may cause brief downtime while the service is restarted. The new size must be
larger than the current size and cannot exceed 1000 GB.

enum: Please create the CNAME record shown above in your DNS provider.
CNAME verified. Certificate resource created in cluster.
CNAME already verified. Certificate already created in cluster.
CNAME record not found. Please ensure the CNAME record is properly configured.

get.summary: Get IP ranges for all nodes grouped by region

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of deployments for an application. Deployments represent the
build and deployment history, showing status, duration, commit information, and who
triggered each deployment. Results are ordered by creation date (newest first).

Retrieve detailed information about a specific deployment including full commit details,
execution timeline, and error information if the deployment failed. Use this endpoint
to check deployment status and troubleshoot failures.

post.description:
Create a new dev session for an application. This scales down the production deployment
and creates a dev container with an AI agent for live editing.
Only one dev session per application is allowed.

get.description: Get the active dev session for an application, if one exists.

delete.description:
Destroy the active dev session for an application. This removes the dev container
and scales the production deployment back up.

post.description:
This endpoint processes infrastructure-as-code definitions to create or update
applications and their associated resources on the Ploi Cloud platform.

## YAML Format

The YAML configuration should follow this structure:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Infrastructure
metadata:
  name: my-app          # Application identifier
  team: 1               # Team ID that owns this infrastructure
spec:
  application:
    type: laravel       # Application type: laravel, nodejs, wordpress
    version: "12"       # Framework version
    label: My App       # Display name (optional)
    tags:               # Tags for organizing apps (optional)
      - production
      - api
    repository:
      url: https://github.com/user/repo
      owner: user
      name: repo
      branch: main
    runtime:
      php_version: 8.4           # PHP version (for PHP apps)
      nodejs_version: "24"       # Node.js version
    commands:
      build:                     # Commands run during build
        - npm ci
        - npm run build
      init:                      # Commands run before app starts
        - php artisan migrate
      start: npm start           # Override start command
    settings:
      health_check_path: /health
      scheduler_enabled: true    # Enable Laravel scheduler
      replicas: 3                # Number of replicas
      memory: 1024Mi             # Memory limit
      scheduled_deletion_at: "2026-12-01T00:00:00Z"  # Optional ISO 8601 timestamp; the application and all of its data will be permanently deleted at this time. Owners receive warning emails 7 and 1 day before. Removing this field clears the schedule.
    php:                         # PHP-specific settings
      extensions:
        - ldap
        - imagick
      settings:
        - memory_limit=512M
        - max_execution_time=60
    security:                    # Optional: Nginx security configuration
      enabled: true              # Enable security headers
      headers:                   # Custom HTTP security headers (optional when enabled=true)
                                 # Example OWASP-recommended headers (from UI "Set default values" button):
        Cache-Control: "no-store, max-age=0"
        Content-Security-Policy: "default-src 'self'; form-action 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests"
        Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp
        Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
        Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
        Permissions-Policy: "accelerometer=(),ambient-light-sensor=(),autoplay=(),battery=(),camera=(),display-capture=(),document-domain=(),encrypted-media=(),execution-while-not-rendered=(),execution-while-out-of-viewport=(),fullscreen=(),gamepad=(),geolocation=(),gyroscope=(),hid=(),idle-detection=(),local-fonts=(),magnetometer=(),microphone=(),midi=(),payment=(),picture-in-picture=(),publickey-credentials-get=(),screen-wake-lock=(),serial=(),speaker-selection=(),usb=(),web-share=(),xr-spatial-tracking=()"
        Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
        Strict-Transport-Security: "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"
        X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
        X-Frame-Options: deny
        X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none
      ssl_protocols: "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"    # SSL/TLS protocols (optional)
      ssl_ciphers: "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:DHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:DHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:DHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305"  # SSL cipher suites (optional)

  domains:
    - domain: app.example.com
    - domain: www.example.com

  secrets:                       # Environment variables
    - key: APP_KEY
      value: base64:your-app-key
    - key: DB_PASSWORD
      value: secret123

  volumes:                       # Persistent volumes
    - name: storage
      mount_path: /var/www/html/storage
      volume_size: 10            # Size in GB

  services:                      # Database/cache services
    - name: database
      type: mysql                # mysql, postgresql, mongodb, redis, valkey, rabbitmq, minio, sftp
      version: "8.0"
      memory: 2Gi
      volume_size: 20Gi
      settings:                  # Service-specific settings
        extensions:
          - postgis

    - name: cache
      type: redis
      version: "7.2"
      memory: 512Mi
      volume_size: 1Gi

    - name: queue-worker        # Worker service
      type: worker
      memory: 1Gi
      command: php artisan queue:work

  container_services:            # Pre-built container services
    - name: pdf-generator
      type: gotenberg            # gotenberg, chrome-headless, clickhouse
      version: "8"
      memory: 1Gi
```

## Query Parameters

- `dry_run` (boolean, default: false) - When true, shows what changes would be made without applying them
- `auto_deploy` (boolean, default: true) - When true, automatically deploys the application after changes

## Example cURL Request

```bash
# Normal deployment
curl -X POST https://api.ploi.cloud/api/v1/infrastructure/apply \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/yaml" \
  --data-binary @ploi.yaml

# Dry run mode - preview changes without applying
curl -X POST "https://api.ploi.cloud/api/v1/infrastructure/apply?dry_run=true" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/yaml" \
  --data-binary @ploi.yaml

# Apply changes without automatic deployment
curl -X POST "https://api.ploi.cloud/api/v1/infrastructure/apply?auto_deploy=false" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/yaml" \
  --data-binary @ploi.yaml
```

## Response Format

```json
{
  "application_id": 123,
  "application_name": "my-app",
  "changes": [
    "Application 'my-app' created",
    "Domain 'app.example.com' added",
    "Secret 'APP_KEY' created",
    "Service 'database' (mysql) created"
  ],
  "structured_changes": {
    "application": { "action": "created", "name": "my-app" },
    "domains": [{ "action": "created", "domain": "app.example.com" }],
    "secrets": [{ "action": "created", "key": "APP_KEY" }],
    "services": [{ "action": "created", "name": "database", "type": "mysql" }]
  },
  "needs_deployment": true,
  "auto_deploy_enabled": true,
  "deployment_id": 456,        // Only present if auto_deploy=true and deployment started
  "dry_run": false,             // Indicates if this was a dry run
  "team": "My Team"
}
```

## Dry Run Mode

When `dry_run=true`:
- No database changes are made
- No resources are created or modified
- Response shows what would be changed with "[DRY RUN] Would..." prefixes
- Deployments are never triggered, even if `auto_deploy=true`
- Use this to validate your YAML and preview changes before applying

get.description:
Retrieve logs from the main application instance. Logs are fetched directly from the
application instance and include stdout/stderr output from the application. Use the tail
parameter to limit the amount of data returned for large log files.

Retrieve build and deployment logs for a specific deployment. Includes output from
git clone, build commands, image build, and deployment operations.
Logs are preserved even after the build process is cleaned up.

Retrieve logs from a specific application service (database, cache, worker, etc.).
Includes service-specific output such as database queries, cache operations, or
worker job processing.

Retrieve all active personal access tokens for the authenticated user. Tokens are
used for API authentication and can have specific scopes or full access. The actual
token values are not included in the response for security reasons.

post.description:
Create a new personal access token for API authentication. The token value is only
shown once during creation and cannot be retrieved again. Store it securely. Tokens
can have specific scopes to limit permissions or use wildcard (*) for full access.
Password verification is required for security.

delete.description:
Permanently revoke a personal access token. This immediately invalidates the token
and any requests using it will fail with 401 Unauthorized. This action cannot be
undone - you'll need to create a new token if needed.

get.description:
Retrieve all teams the authenticated user belongs to, including teams they own and teams
where they are a member. Each team represents an isolated workspace with its own applications
and members. The response includes aggregate counts for applications and members.

Retrieve detailed information about a specific team. Only accessible to team members.
Shows team information, member count, application count, and the current user's role
within the team.

enum: Cannot delete network with active members. Remove all applications first.
Application added to network. NetworkPolicy will be updated on next deployment.
Application removed from network. NetworkPolicy will be updated on next deployment.

get.description:
Retrieve the current team's credit balance and PDF API usage statistics.
This endpoint does not consume credits.

Verify that the PDF conversion service is available and operational.
This endpoint does not consume credits.

post.description:
Convert a web page to PDF format. The URL must be publicly accessible.
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Convert HTML content to PDF format. Supports inline CSS and images (base64 encoded).
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Convert Markdown content to PDF format with automatic styling.
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Convert Microsoft Office documents (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and other formats to PDF.
Supported formats: .docx, .doc, .xlsx, .xls, .pptx, .ppt, .odt, .ods, .odp, .rtf, .txt, .csv
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Combine multiple PDF files into a single PDF document.
Files are merged in the order they are provided.
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Capture a screenshot of a web page. Returns PNG or JPEG image.
Consumes 1 credit per request.

Capture a screenshot of HTML content. Returns PNG or JPEG image.
Consumes 1 credit per request.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information about the authenticated user including their profile,
current team context, and all teams they belong to. The avatar URL is generated
using Gravatar based on the user's email address.

put.description:
Switch the authenticated user's current team context. All subsequent API requests
will operate within the context of the selected team. The user must be a member
of the team to switch to it.

StoreApplicationServiceRequest.description:
Store Application Service Request

Validates the creation of a new application service (database, cache, worker, etc.).
Includes service-specific validation rules based on the service type.
StoreApplicationServiceRequest.title: StoreApplicationServiceRequest

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
